reference_scope: aMule runtime behavior; completed ed2k-refactor tracker
current_surface: The completed ed2k-refactor tracker verifies protocol alignment, while the AR00 audit shows the next gap is runtime behavior: source maintenance, server/Kad cadence, peer lifecycle, queue reask, LowID state, identity, transfer pacing, upload cooperation, and credits.
target_or_prune_reason: Use aMule as the primary runtime behavior reference and use the completed refactor tracker as the local protocol baseline.
user_impact: Future work focuses on practical download reliability instead of repeating protocol-parity claims.

reference_scope: aMule user hash and credit identity persistence
current_surface: aria2-next now stores a normalized hidden ed2k-client-hash in request/session state instead of deriving identity from the per-process DownloadEngine sessionId.
target_or_prune_reason: Persist or restore ED2K identity through aria2-next-native hidden state so remote queues and credits are not reset unnecessarily.
user_impact: Peers can recognize the client across restarts where supported.

reference_scope: aMule known file, part file, server, Kad, and client runtime state
current_surface: aria2-next keeps AR10 runtime metadata limited to hidden session/control-file state: ED2K client hash plus existing server, Kad, shared-file, and credit state.
target_or_prune_reason: Keep aria2-next ownership and store only runtime metadata needed for reliable scheduling, source discovery, identity, and credits.
user_impact: No legacy database stack is introduced.

reference_scope: aMule source limits, rare-file behavior, and source quality handling
current_surface: PeerState and ed2k_policy.* now classify lifecycle state and can apply an active source cap while preserving source-origin, fail-count, queued, queue-rank, and availability ordering.
target_or_prune_reason: Keep source scheduling aligned with aria2-next concurrency while avoiding blind connection churn.
user_impact: Large source lists stop causing noisy or low-value connection churn.

reference_scope: aMule DeadSourceList and source removal rules
current_surface: PeerState retry and dead flags now have explicit expiry through expireEd2kDeadSources, called before peer scheduling.
target_or_prune_reason: Expire dead and no-file state after bounded retry time so bad sources are not re-added in tight loops and stale dead entries can recover later.
user_impact: Bad sources stop being retried too aggressively while stale entries can become eligible again.

reference_scope: aMule no-needed-parts source state
current_surface: PeerState partStatus and outOfParts state now map to a NO_NEEDED_PARTS lifecycle and are excluded from normal connect selection without being marked dead.
target_or_prune_reason: Represent peers that are valid but temporarily useless separately from dead or failed peers.
user_impact: Useful peers are not discarded, and useless peers do not consume active slots.

reference_scope: aMule server TCP and UDP reask timing
current_surface: Ed2kCommand server path and Ed2kKadCommand server UDP path
target_or_prune_reason: TCP source reasks now use an aMule-style 800 second interval, and UDP source reasks use an aMule-style 1300 second per-server interval.
user_impact: Servers are queried responsibly and source refresh becomes predictable without noisy steady-state polling.

reference_scope: aMule Server and ServerList metadata
target_or_prune_reason: ServerState now preserves source response freshness, source count, UDP source request timing, UDP flags, large-file support, and retry state for source request decisions.
user_impact: Server discovery uses compatible request forms and avoids recently useful or backoff-bound servers.

reference_scope: aMule Kad routing and bootstrap maintenance
target_or_prune_reason: Kad routing maintenance keeps bounded bootstrap and refresh behavior, and useful routing state now includes replacement contacts for scheduling decisions.
user_impact: Kad can remain a durable source channel instead of depending only on first-run bootstrap.

reference_scope: aMule Kad source search and source publish loops
target_or_prune_reason: Kad source search now has persisted per-file cadence state with aMule-style increasing hourly backoff, source-count gating, active-traversal gating, and existing publish/firewalled maintenance state persistence.
user_impact: Low-source files get continued bounded Kad discovery when routing state is usable.

reference_scope: aMule CUpDownClient download states
target_or_prune_reason: Peer lifecycle classification now distinguishes useful, connecting, queued, downloading, callback-waiting, no-needed-parts, dead, retry, no-file, and cancelled states for policy decisions.
user_impact: Scheduler behavior is easier to reason about, and later RPC/status work can reuse the same lifecycle truth.

reference_scope: aMule DownloadQueue and PartFile processing
target_or_prune_reason: Policy now returns explicit wait, connect, reask, callback, retry, skip, and expire action types instead of exposing only connectable peers.
user_impact: Queued reask, LowID callback, and retry work can attach to one deterministic policy boundary.

reference_scope: aMule UDPReaskForDownload, UDPReaskACK, UDPReaskFNF
current_surface: Ed2kKadCommand OP_REASKACK parser and PeerState queue fields
target_or_prune_reason: Queued peers now keep UDP metadata, last/next reask time, pending state, and receive bounded OP_REASKFILEPING from the existing UDP command loop.
user_impact: Queued sources can advance instead of sitting at zero bytes indefinitely.

reference_scope: aMule UDP queue-full and file-not-found handling
target_or_prune_reason: OP_REASKACK refreshes rank and availability, OP_QUEUEFULL becomes temporary retry state, and OP_FILENOTFOUND remains dead/no-file source evidence.
user_impact: The client avoids both permanent source loss and tight retry loops.

reference_scope: aMule server callback and firewalled source handling
current_surface: PeerState now carries LowIdCallbackState plus compatibility mirrors for callbackRequested and callbackImpossible.
target_or_prune_reason: Server-mediated LowID callback now tracks requested, accepted, failed, timed-out, impossible, and completed states, and scheduler policy blocks LowID direct connects until callback makes a reachable endpoint available.
user_impact: LowID sources do not poison normal direct-connect scheduling and callback failures recover through bounded expiry.

reference_scope: aMule Kad buddy and direct callback behavior
current_surface: aria2-next has Kad source/search/firewall maintenance but no native buddy owner, direct UDP callback verification owner, or advertised direct callback capability.
target_or_prune_reason: Keep server-mediated callback as the supported LowID path for this checkpoint. Preserve Kad/firewalled metadata and leave buddy/direct callback unadvertised until a later owner implements buddy identity, callback routing, timeout, and security checks.
user_impact: LowID limitations are explicit; firewalled peers may still need manual port reachability or future buddy/direct callback work.

reference_scope: aMule block request timing and timeout handling
target_or_prune_reason: Request windows now timestamp outbound ranges, remove completed ranges, cancel stalled SegmentMan ownership, and send OP_CANCELTRANSFER through the peer command.
user_impact: Stalled peers stop holding useful ranges forever and re-enter bounded retry state.

reference_scope: aMule corrupt block and stalled transfer recovery
target_or_prune_reason: Existing disk/hash/AICH safety remains the corruption boundary; stalled no-progress peers now clear requested ranges and retry predictably.
user_impact: Valid data remains safe while failed peers recover without blocking other sources.

reference_scope: aMule upload queue and reask response behavior
target_or_prune_reason: Upload queue cooperation now keeps TCP slot/rank behavior and UDP reask responses aligned: waiting peers receive rank ACKs, active uploaders receive rank-zero ACKs, and unknown UDP reask senders receive queue-full instead of a misleading rank-zero ACK.
user_impact: Remote peers get truthful upload queue responses from aria2-next.

reference_scope: aMule ClientCredits and secure-ident limits
target_or_prune_reason: Practical uploaded/downloaded credit counters persist through aria2-next session state and influence waiting upload queue order while secure-ident remains unadvertised.
user_impact: Credit behavior improves fairness without false identity verification claims.

reference_scope: aMule wx UI, web UI, EC remote control, chat, friends, comments, preview, collections
target_or_prune_reason: Prune legacy application surfaces that do not belong in aria2-next. They are application-shell features, not download-runtime behavior required for reliable ED2K transfers.
user_impact: Scope stays focused on download reliability.

reference_scope: aMule UPnP/NAT helpers and port tests
current_surface: aria2-next listen-port options and existing manual firewall guidance
target_or_prune_reason: Replace aMule UPnP/NAT helper stacks with existing aria2-next listen-port ownership and documentation. Do not add a router-control dependency for ED2K hardening.
user_impact: No extra NAT dependency or background router control is introduced; users remain responsible for firewall and port forwarding.

reference_scope: aMule peer capability negotiation; completed ed2k-refactor capability ledger
target_or_prune_reason: Local ED2K capability construction still advertises only implemented send, receive, state, error, and fallback paths: AICH, Unicode, compression, Source Exchange, extended requests, and large-file support remain represented, while multipacket, extended multipacket, crypt, secure-ident, Kad, and file identifier capabilities stay unadvertised.
user_impact: Peers do not select unsupported local flows.

reference_scope: aria2-next RPC patterns and Motrix Next integration
target_or_prune_reason: Expose hardened runtime state through stable aggregate ED2K fields. lowIdPeerCount and callbackWaitingPeerCount were added alongside existing peer, queued, accepted, dead, server, Kad, and sharing counters.
user_impact: Integrators can distinguish LowID callback waiting from ordinary queued or dead-source conditions without relying on per-peer internals.
